judge.blindness: Each verdict produced by an independent subagent given ONLY the buildJudgePrompt() output for its case (claim criteria + user turn + assistant response), with expected labels, rationale, kind, tier, and length count stripped; instructed to judge from that text alone.

result.interpretation: The judge CAUGHT the real natural unsound (sc2: 240 chars > the stated 200 limit) and code agrees (deterministic length check confirms 240). But it FAILED calibration overall by judging sc4 unsound for a 'one paragraph' structural violation while accepting the identical answer-paragraph-plus-요약-line structure in sc1 and sc3 — an inconsistent application of an ambiguous deterministic criterion. The calibration discipline caught an unreliable judge BEFORE it was trusted.

harmonyFinding: The judge is reliable where the criterion is unambiguous (length: judge and code both flag sc2) and unreliable where a mechanical criterion is ambiguous (structure: inconsistent across sc1/sc3/sc4). Fix direction (empirical D2 boundary): move deterministic format facets (length, bullet/list presence, required-line presence, structure) to CODE; leave only genuinely semantic facets (answered_substantively, no_fabrication) to the intelligence judge. This is the code+intelligence harmony the maintainer flagged, now grounded in data.

verdicts.evidence: Korean prose, no lists, ends with 요약 line, content correct; no length limit stated.
Counted the response as exceeding the stated 200-char limit (code confirms 240) — constraint (4) dropped.
Korean prose, no lists, 요약 line, content correct; no length limit.

Judged the answer-paragraph + 요약-line as 'two paragraphs' violating 'one paragraph' — INCONSISTENT with its own acceptance of the same structure in sc1/sc3. Code confirms sc4 length 142 <= 200, so length was not the issue.
